formal_event: Issuer and rating disclosures state that since 2014 the company no longer participated in new government-project investment-financing and shifted to fiscal-funded entrusted project management.
continued_function: The company moved away from new government-project investment-financing toward budget-funded project management; old and current disclosures both state the post-2014 entrusted-management model and the 2018 announcement shows part of the 2014 MTN being recognized as government-repayment-responsibility debt and included in the fiscal budget for replacement.
ambiguity_note: The company still performs public infrastructure and project-management functions and holds large public-welfare assets so the label depends on distinguishing budgetary project management from off-budget government financing.
caveat: This is a fiscal-substitution case rather than an exit-list case; final confidence should be raised only after collecting the original Guangzhou 2008 reform document or additional 2014 issuance materials.
notes: Use as second human-validated pilot label and contrast case against Xi'an Hi-tech.

platform: 福州城市建设投资集团有限公司
company_name: 福州城市建设投资集团有限公司
formal_event: Prospectus states that the company does not undertake government financing functions and that new debt after January 1 2015 is not local government debt.
continued_function: Formal no-government-financing language coexists with a major reorganization of public-project finance. The issuer remains a leading Fuzhou infrastructure, shantytown-renovation, affordable-housing, municipal代建, and area-development platform, but the prospectus documents that after 2011 municipal project funding shifted from issuer financing with fiscal repayment to full fiscal appropriation and management-fee settlement. PPP projects, government-purchase-service projects that were later adjusted, fiscal subsidies, government receivables, and rating evidence of continuing fiscal support all point to functional transfer rather than substantive disappearance.
ambiguity_note: Because the same legal issuer continues to carry many public development tasks, a narrower reading would code the case as nominal exit. The functional-transfer label rests on the documented shift in project financing and settlement rules rather than on the disappearance of the function.
caveat: No independent platform-list exit document has been collected. Historical-capacity metrics for this added Fuzhou target still need to be merged into the candidate capacity file.
notes: New Fujian provincial-capital validated case; useful as a formal fiscal-substitution contrast to nominal-exit cases.

continued_function: The same legal issuer was converted from a development-oriented state-owned assets investment group into a state-capital investment and operation platform. Direct urban infrastructure and area-development language disappeared from the issuer business scope, while municipal infrastructure functions continued through equity stakes in Fuzhou Chengtou, Fuzhou Zuohai, and other city platforms and through the Mindu infrastructure fund. This is a transfer into portfolio and fund channels rather than substantive disappearance.
ambiguity_note: If the coding rule focused only on the issuer direct business scope, the 2022 scope change could support substantive exit. The functional-transfer label rests on evidence that infrastructure and public investment remained inside the issuer institutional orbit through equity ownership and fund-based investment.
caveat: No independent platform-list exit document has been collected. The formal event is a 2022 SASAC-approved name, scope, and asset reorganization rather than an explicit financing-platform-list exit notice.
notes: New Fuzhou state-capital-operation case; useful paired with the existing Fuzhou Chengtou case to show within-city differentiation across platforms.

continued_function: The 2023 restructuring created an industrial-investment holding group and contains formal government-financing-function stripping language. But the same group system continues to show traffic infrastructure construction, land整理, fiscal-payment arrangements, government-linked receivables, and repurchase-payment risk. The corporate label changed more clearly than the quasi-fiscal development function, so this is coded as nominal exit.
ambiguity_note: The asset transfers to transportation, railway, automobile, and commercial-management entities support a partial functional-transfer interpretation. The final label remains nominal because the current evidence still places major land, traffic, fiscal-support, and government-repurchase functions inside the issuer or its consolidated group.
caveat: The original 2023 municipal reorganization notice has not been collected directly. The formal event and functional evidence rely on the 2026 prospectus and legal opinion.
notes: New Jiangsu boundary case; useful for distinguishing nominal exit from partial functional transfer.

platform: 常熟市投资控股集团有限公司

This pattern fits functional transfer through policy business and state-capital channels rather than substantive exit or pure nominal exit.

caveat: Only the prospectus and legal opinion were locally extracted in this pass. A rating report or local government document should be collected before raising confidence.
notes: New Jiangsu county-level functional-transfer case; useful counterweight to high-confidence nominal cases.
